---
title: "Resource Library"
listing:
  id: resource-listing
  type: grid
  contents: "*/*.qmd"
  filter-ui: true
  sort-ui: true
  categories: true
  sort: "date desc"
  fields: [image, title, description, categories, date]
  page-size: 24
---

The Methods Hub resource library is a searchable collection of individual pages covering research methods in the social sciences. Each page addresses one task or concept — working through a page takes roughly 20–40 minutes. Resources are written at BA level by default, with MA/PhD extensions where relevant.

Use the filters below to browse by topic, level, or content type.

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex justify-content-end mb-2">
  <div class="btn-group btn-group-sm" role="group" aria-label="View toggle">
    <button id="view-btn-grid" type="button" class="btn btn-outline-secondary active">
      <i class="fa-solid fa-grip"></i> Grid
    </button>
    <button id="view-btn-list" type="button" class="btn btn-outline-secondary">
      <i class="fa-solid fa-list"></i> List
    </button>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {#resource-listing}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  function setView(view) {
    var el = document.getElementById('listing-resource-listing');
    var btnGrid = document.getElementById('view-btn-grid');
    var btnList = document.getElementById('view-btn-list');
    if (!el || !btnGrid || !btnList) return;
    var isGrid = view === 'grid';
    el.classList.toggle('list-mode', !isGrid);
    btnGrid.classList.toggle('active', isGrid);
    btnList.classList.toggle('active', !isGrid);
    try { localStorage.setItem('resourceView', view); } catch (e) {}
  }

  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
    var saved;
    try { saved = localStorage.getItem('resourceView'); } catch (e) {}
    setView(saved || 'grid');
    document.getElementById('view-btn-grid').addEventListener('click', function () { setView('grid'); });
    document.getElementById('view-btn-list').addEventListener('click', function () { setView('list'); });
  });
}());
</script>
```
